prose:
Method–provenance block (reproducible calculation details)

• Property calculated  Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) for nitrobenzene; most electrophilic atom (C₄) f⁺ = 0.4964178 (unit-less).
• Level of theory      DFT, tier ‘standard’: B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (PySCF).
• Geometry provenance  User-supplied file ‘nitrobenzene.xyz’; single-point charges on this fixed geometry for the neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge –1, multiplicity 2) states — a DFT//geometry-from-file protocol (no further optimisation).
• Solvent / phase      Gas phase (no implicit solvent model).
• Software             PySCF (via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill).
• Charge & mult.       Neutral singlet (0,1); cation doublet (+1,2); anion doublet (-1,2).
• Convergence          All three SCF calculations converged; integrity check passed.
  Warnings surfaced verbatim below (all flagged as “warning” severity but with ok = true).

Verbatim engine warnings
– fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
– schema_header: common header present

Reproducibility
Command executed (from result JSON):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py \
    fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp \
    /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz \
    --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpce8v0dp6/live.json
The live PySCF log was streamed to a fukui_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory while the job ran; consult it for full SCF and charge analyses. The JSON output (“live.json”) contains the complete set of charges, Fukui indices, and integrity checks.

Headline result
• Most electrophilic site: atom 4 (C, index 3) with f⁺ = 0.4964178
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C, index 4) with f⁻ = 0.1072882

Computed result — no comparison to experiment is supplied here.
